appliedUpdates.description: Created .factory/library/harness-api-knowledge.md with discovered knowledge: bench_data ignore rules, TG_SIDECAR_SCRIPT mock workaround, JSON_OUTPUT_VERSION coupling risk, and example artifact regeneration notes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the 'User Preferences' section to include sidecar_used in the unified JSON envelope field list. Current text says 'version, routing_backend, routing_reason' but the implementation and architecture docs include all four: version, routing_backend, routing_reason, sidecar_used.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features unify-json-envelope and schema-compat-test both noted the discrepancy. The implementation correctly includes all 4 fields, but AGENTS.md could mislead future workers into omitting sidecar_used. Two independent reviewers flagged this same gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a coding convention note: 'JSON_OUTPUT_VERSION constant lives in main.rs. Any code emitting version fields (including backend_ast.rs) must reference this constant, not hardcode the value.' This prevents version drift if the constant is ever bumped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature unify-json-envelope review found backend_ast.rs hardcodes version: 1 at two locations instead of using the JSON_OUTPUT_VERSION constant from main.rs. No compile-time safeguard exists.

rejectedObservations.observation: No SKILL.md file exists for rust-worker skill in .factory/skills/
rejectedObservations.reason: Skills are built-in/personal droid configurations, not necessarily repo-level files. The rust-worker skill is defined at the personal droid level, not in the project .factory/skills/ directory.

rejectedObservations.observation: sidecar_used could be added to assert_common_envelope helper in test_schema_compat.rs
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking code quality suggestion. The field IS validated per-shape in each assert function, so coverage is complete. Centralizing it would be nice but is not a correctness issue.

rejectedObservations.observation: Example JSON regeneration is not reproducible from committed state (no generation script)
rejectedObservations.reason: Acceptable for a documentation feature. Examples serve as shape references for the schema compat test, not as runnable CI fixtures. The docs include command templates for regeneration.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker correctly followed TDD convention (positive observation)
rejectedObservations.reason: No action needed — this is a positive observation confirming process compliance, not a gap.
